creator: empty.japan
caption:
It’s been a while since I’ve had a Kaiseki dinner.

Kaiseki isn’t just a multi-course meal, it’s a reflection of Japan’s seasons. Each dish is crafted with peak seasonal ingredients, bringing out the richest, fullest flavors.

The best Kaiseki experiences aren’t always in famous or pricey places. For example, this one was only $70, yet it became one of my favorites - far more memorable than a $250 Kaiseki I once had. 

Have you tried Kaiseki in Japan? Was it worth the price?

📍Kintarou Onsen Hotel, Toyama
